items: Multi-space support: open and manage multiple spaces in separate windows. AI, file, database, and git operations stay scoped to the active window.
Open external Markdown files from Finder into standalone Glyph windows. Glyph registers .md, .markdown, and .mdown associations on macOS.
Default accent switched from cerulean to neutral across the settings constant, appearance fallback, and appearance settings pane initializer.

The default light and dark themes give the editor its own surface, separate from the sidebar. Light mode text uses a deeper warm ink. Dark mode text uses solid off-white instead of translucent white. Dark backgrounds shifted from flat gray to a cooler indigo. Borders softened to match.
Paper grain texture shows across the empty editor and large background areas.
Sidebar and editor surfaces blend more smoothly into each other.
Window top and bottom edges carry a faint gradient that reads as light from above.

Floating panels in light mode cast soft warm shadows. In dark mode they cast deep cool shadows with a thin top highlight. Dark popovers and tooltips now float with an inner highlight and outer glow instead of reading flat.
Database toolbar New Note button uses a plus icon and a plain chip button, consistent with the rest of the toolbar.

Settings toggle rows are fully clickable. You can click the label or the row itself to flip the switch. Hover shows a subtle highlight. Clicks still pass through to text fields, links, and the toggle's own label. Disabled toggles show a not-allowed cursor.
External file and change events no longer leak across spaces. Each event targets the correct window and space.
Settings sidebar groups tabs under Application and Workspace headings.
Release builds block the right-click context menu and F5 / Cmd+R / Ctrl+R reload shortcuts. Dev mode is unaffected.
Safer atomic writes when creating new files to avoid collisions.
Tests for space-scoped settings.

OS notifications for index rebuilds and AI chat results. The Tauri notification plugin, dependency, and default capability are removed. In-app toasts remain for copy errors, failed file actions, and extraction failures.
Week strip shows compact workload signals for task count, note activity, daily notes, and selected-day overdue tasks
Tasks now has a dedicated Home subview with Today, Overdue, Inbox, No date, By note, By tag, All, search, refresh, grouping, checkbox toggles, scheduling, and note opening
Global Tasks command opens Home with the Tasks subview selected
Quick Task floating window captures tasks from anywhere with text, scheduled date, due date, Priority, and Inbox controls
Default Quick Task shortcut is Cmd/Ctrl+Alt+Shift+T
Task composer now supports Scheduled date, Due date, Today, Priority, Inbox, and source note controls
Tasks now loads unchecked tasks across the whole space instead of the current calendar range
By note shows unchecked unscheduled note rows grouped by folder, with file names and plain task counts
All shows scheduled tasks inline and unscheduled tasks as folder-grouped note rows
Task composer in Home and Tasks shares the same capture flow, so due dates are written consistently
Home now shows a centered Home and Tasks switch below the main tab bar when tabs are open
Sidebar search placeholder changed to Quick Actions, with the old space-name search icon removed
Active navigation rows now use clearer text weight and icon stroke across the sidebar, file tree, and Folio
Task rings use consistent sizing variables so progress indicators stay centered
Tags in table view and All Notes now use consistent styling
Database board cards no longer show folder paths in the footer
Task progress rings in the file tree, database, and Folio now match the centered geometry used by All Notes
Folder icons no longer get a separate hover background inside file tree rows
Separate Unscheduled tab in Tasks
Sidebar Tasks button
Folder footer from database board cards
More than 300 lines of dead CSS and TSX
Beautiful Tags is available as an advanced setting. It stays off by default and gives tags predefined smart icons when you enable it
Settings search helps you find preferences from the sidebar or command palette, then highlights the matching control
The AI composer suggests the active markdown file as context when the AI panel is open. Click the muted file chip to attach it
Database text cells, linked-notes cells, and frontmatter text properties now support [[ note autocomplete
Tag icons can use automatic defaults, and a centered picker lets you change each tag icon
Folders, files, and tags now share the same icon and color picker flow for appearance customization
Tag names and counts open the tagged notes, while the tag icon opens customization
All Notes cards are smaller and cleaner, with neutral surfaces, footer metadata, stronger title and preview hierarchy, task progress, and calmer dark-mode shadows
The built-in Linked Notes database column is editable, and manual entries or autocomplete selections save through the normal cell update flow
Linked-note edits persist to linked_notes frontmatter as wikilinks, and refreshed rows include unresolved wikilink titles
Displayed frontmatter and property wikilinks are clickable in the editor and info sidebar
Table and frontmatter autocomplete menus now match the editor wikilink selector
Opening the note info sidebar still closes the AI sidebar without triggering React's nested state update warning
Database and frontmatter wikilink autocomplete menus no longer clip inside table cells
All Notes removes content-type badges, random card colors, the card density switcher, and the note count in the header
Pin Folio notes and files from the right-click menu. Pinned items stay at the top under a compact Pinned heading
Folio note cards show your custom icon, or the default note icon when you have not picked one
Create database notes inside a board lane or grouped table. Glyph applies the lane or group value to the new note, including status, tag, priority, checkbox, or select
Database view options include quick filter and sort preset chips for overdue, high priority, owner is me, active project, and other common views
Priority works as a YAML property with No, Low, Medium, and High values across note properties, database cells, and boards
Database rows support field defaults, including status, priority, checkbox, and schema-backed property defaults
Board workflows let you add, rename, reorder, and color lanes for status and multi-select fields
Breadcrumb right-click menus can copy paths, reveal items in the file tree, open folders, and show files in Finder
Home uses a wider dashboard layout with a date header, week overview, Notes panel, Tasks panel, and quick actions for today's daily note and new tasks
Home buttons match the sidebar accent style, and note tags sit inline with note titles as clean pills
Folio note rows use tighter spacing, right-aligned icons, fixed thumbnail and tag overflow, and task rings aligned with the action area
The selected Folio note uses a 2px accent rail and a lighter selected tint for a cleaner reading list
Folio remembers your sort choice between sessions
Custom appearance colors tint Folio note titles and non-markdown file names
Markdown notes use the new default Note icon anywhere the shared file icon wrapper appears
Tag pills use flatter 4px corners across database boards, database tables, note properties, inline editor tags, tasks, and model details
Status fields use nine choices: Backlog, Todo, In progress, In review, Blocked, Waiting, Done, Canceled, and Archived
Existing status values map into the new status set, so older YAML still lands in the closest current status
Kanban cards show status, priority, and tags together in the metadata row with the same colored text treatment
Kanban column headings use matching icons for status, priority, tags, and custom grouped fields
Configured empty board lanes stay visible, so your workflow structure does not disappear when a column has no cards
Moving a card to a workflow lane replaces the old status or select value. Glyph keeps your manual card order inside each lane
Done and Archived lanes carry workflow state and use distinct board styling
Breadcrumbs use the same folder and file icons, colors, and custom appearance choices that you set in the file tree
Long breadcrumb paths collapse middle items behind an ellipsis, and breadcrumb menu items expose full paths on hover
The editor actions button opens the shared native popup menu for Info, Local graph, Edit, Preview, and Raw
Close Tab closes the active tab when one exists, and closes the app window when no tabs remain
Daily Notes settings opens Space settings instead of General settings
Quick create in databases inserts the new row at once and refreshes in the background, so you do not hit a blocking Loading rows message
Glyph removes beta labels from Folio Mode and Git settings after moving both areas into the main settings experience
Glyph removes old beta badge plumbing and unused badge styles from settings
Glyph replaces the custom animated editor actions panel with the shared native popup menu
Added a reusable native menu helper for Tauri popup menus
Added a Tauri command that opens the native Space menu from the sidebar space button
File tree, Folio, and database board right-click menus now use native macOS/Tauri popup menus
Icon and color appearance actions now live in native submenus
Database folder selection now uses the native macOS folder picker
Settings and database pickers now use native select controls
Home now opens as a centered weekly panel with notes, tasks, stable sizing, and native-style controls
Database board cards, lane buttons, handles, and color swatches now use calmer hover states
The main app window now has a 680 x 460 minimum size
Escape and Command + [ now close Settings and return to the main app view
The editor actions dropdown now opens and closes faster
The active editor title rename input no longer shows the shared focus underline
Fixed Pi provider launches when Tauri starts the CLI without the Node install directory in the child environment
Open Calendar now lives under Open Home, and Close AI Panel now lives under Toggle AI Panel
Removed the main-window AI Agent tab, command path, component, helper, manifest entry, and tab-only CSS while keeping the right-side AI panel intact
Removed old Radix context-menu wrappers, stale context-menu CSS, unused test mocks, and the FileTreeAppearanceMenu component
Removed unused shadcn primitive exports from dialog, dropdown-menu, popover, scroll-area, and table
Removed unused source exports, internal-only public types, dead declarations, unused icon wrappers, and the stale release-notes type stub
Removed unused dependencies: @hookform/resolvers, @tiptap/extension-underline, cmdk, react-day-picker, react-hook-form, react-resizable-panels, and zod
Removed the old month-picker code, unused calendar components, stale calendar CSS, and SettingsSegmented styles
Removed Reload and Save from the Markdown editor actions menu
Added Pi as an agent provider
Added Claude as an agent provider through `claude -p`, following Anthropic's terms of service
Added Show in Finder to note context menus in the file tree and Folio
Added an embedded Mermaid canvas with pan, fit, and Cmd-wheel zoom
Quick Note can append to any note in the current space from a folder-grouped picker
Active TOC dashes now use the accent color, and the collapsed TOC shows only the dashes
The file tree Notes header now includes expand-all and collapse-all controls
Mermaid now renders sanitized SVG through beautiful-mermaid and switches between source and preview when you click the block
The sidebar now collapses below 760px and gives the main area the freed space
The expand-sidebar button now disables while the window is too narrow to reopen the sidebar
The floating TOC now follows the active heading while you scroll
Delete and Backspace now open the confirmation flow for focused files, folders, and selected Folio notes without affecting text inputs or rename fields
The file tree now highlights the open file without also highlighting its parent folder
Removed the Mermaid Play/Stop overlay and bundled mermaid package
Fixed OpenCode event streams closing before completion
You can now rename files by clicking the title in the editor
Database relation and multi-select pills now use the app accent color instead of per-value colors
Sidebar, Folio, database, and view rename fields now edit like plain text instead of boxed inputs
YAML and frontmatter editing now feels like part of the editor
About settings now has cleaner copy and less unused code
Restored and simplified the global app scrollbar styles
Fixed Folio note renaming
Folio search and sort controls now respect the collapsed-sidebar space
Editor tab bar controls now start to the right of the floating sidebar button
Removed the animation when switching between the command palette and search
Removed the folder name and note count from the top of Folio mode
Removed the note count from the sidebar All Notes item, including fetching, storage, announcements, rendering, and styling
Removed the duplicate Support and Diagnostics section from About settings
The editor feels cleaner and faster, with quicker slash commands and AI responses
Tags now use your theme accent color for a more consistent workspace
The sidebar has a cleaner layout with less visual noise
Glyph now opens file previews in the default macOS app
Table tag and value suggestions now stay above neighboring cells while you edit them
Right sidebar resizing now tracks the pointer more cleanly
Removed automatic updates and the related General settings text
Removed the task progress setting. Task progress indicators stay on
Removed the database note count setting and the note total from database headers
Removed HTML export, note printing, Zen Mode, and markdown saving from the web view
Removed Git sync status from the sidebar footer
Removed the delightful Glyph setting and related code
Trimmed the built-in theme list
